### Post by sierra_1 on 2009-09-25
Hi! :(

My prompt used to read:
sierra@sierra:~$

It now reads:
sierra@localhost:~$

$ gksudo gedit /etc/hosts

#Generated by Moblin Imag Creator #
127.0.0.1   localhost.localdomain    localhost
#The following lines are desirable for IPv6 cpable hosts

::1 ip6 localhost ip6-loopback
fe00::0 ip6-localnet
fe00::0 ip6-mcastprefix
fe00::1 ip6-allnodes
fe00::2 ip6-allrouters
fe00::3 ip6-allhosts

I also saw a  message about it can't authenticate, but can't duplicate the message at this time.

I notice the time is wrong to, although the timezone is right.

Please help...

---

### Post by erilidon on 2009-09-25
```
gksudo gedit /etc/hostname
```

the hostname file will open, change it to whatever you want. Not sure about the time problem.

### Post by sierra_1 on 2009-09-25
$ gksudo edit /etc/hosts

127.0.0.1 localhost
127.0.0.1 sierra.localdomain local host
